title: armeria maritima subsp. arctica
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic Sea Thrift, is a subspecies of the Sea Thrift plant, native to the Arctic tundra regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Arctic Sea Thrift include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections. Arctic Sea Thrift can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
